content_markdown: Which of the following gives the resistances of an ideal ammeter and an ideal voltmeter?

diagram_metadata.description: A table showing four combinations of resistance values (infinite or zero) for an ideal ammeter and an ideal voltmeter.

options.label: A
options.text: Resistance of ideal ammeter: infinite; Resistance of ideal voltmeter: infinite

options.label: B
options.text: Resistance of ideal ammeter: zero; Resistance of ideal voltmeter: infinite

options.label: C
options.text: Resistance of ideal ammeter: infinite; Resistance of ideal voltmeter: zero

options.label: D
options.text: Resistance of ideal ammeter: zero; Resistance of ideal voltmeter: zero

vibe_explanation: An ideal ammeter is connected in series and should not affect the current it measures, so it must have zero resistance. An ideal voltmeter is connected in parallel and should not draw any current from the circuit, so it must have infinite resistance.
